response: THE FIRST INSTALLATION EMPLOYS A PARAMETRIC EQUATION TO GENERATE A DYNAMIC GEOMETRIC PATTERN ON A LARGE SCREEN. THE EQUATIONS FOR X(T) AND Y(T) ARE DEFINED AS X(T) = COS(AT + Φ) + COS(BT) AND Y(T) = SIN(AT + Φ) + SIN(BT), RESPECTIVELY. FOR THE PATTERN TO FORM A CLOSED LOOP, THE FUNCTION MUST REPEAT ITSELF AFTER A CERTAIN PERIOD T. THIS OCCURS WHEN THE TOTAL PHASE CHANGE OF THE PARAMETRIC EQUATIONS AFTER TIME T IS A MULTIPLE OF 2π. THE CONDITIONS ON A, B, AND Φ ARE THAT THE RATIO OF A TO B MUST BE A RATIONAL NUMBER, SAY A/B = P/Q, WHERE P AND Q ARE INTEGERS. THIS ENSURES THAT THE TRAJECTORY CLOSURES AFTER A COMMON PERIOD T = 2πQ/A = 2πP/B. THE PHASE SHIFT Φ CAN BE ANY REAL NUMBER, BUT IT AFFECTS THE STARTING POINT OF THE LOOP, NOT ITS CLOSURE. THE PERIOD T OF THE CLOSED LOOP IS DETERMINED BY THE LEAST COMMON MULTIPLE OF THE INDIVIDUAL PERIODS OF THE COSINE AND SINE TERMS. THE INDIVIDUAL PERIODS ARE 2π/A AND 2π/B, SO T = 2π * LCM(1/A, 1/B) = 2π * LCM(Q, P)/PQ. THIS ENSURES THE PATTERN REPEATS AFTER TIME T, FORMING A CLOSED LOOP. THE SECOND INSTALLATION INVOLVES A 3D SURFACE DEFINED BY THE IMPLICIT FUNCTION F(X, Y, Z) = X² + Y² + Z² + λ(XY + YZ + ZX) - 1 = 0. THE PARAMETER λ INFLUENCES THE SHAPE AND TOPOLOGY OF THE SURFACE. AS λ CHANGES, THE SURFACE CAN TRANSFORM FROM A SPHERE INTO MORE COMPLEX SHAPES. THE CRITICAL VALUES OF λ OCCUR WHEN THE EQUATION UNDERGOES A BIFURCATION, LEADING TO A CHANGE IN THE NUMBER OF CONNECTED COMPONENTS OR HOLE STRUCTURES. THIS HAPPENS WHEN THE EIGENVALUES OF THE QUADRATIC FORM ASSOCIATED WITH THE FUNCTION CHANGE SIGN
